title: Add Wishlist Module persistence
verify: Wishlist Module is registered without modifying Medusa Core/Product/Customer tables.
Migration creates wishlist item persistence with unique customer_id/product_id constraint and customer-list index.
Real PostgreSQL create/read/delete and fresh-process read succeed.
Concurrent duplicate create converges on one durable record or a recoverable unique conflict.
evidence_required: Backend typecheck and Memory Bank lint output.
constraints: Use one custom Medusa module/model and generated migration.
Store only customer_id and product_id plus standard timestamps.
Do not add cross-module database foreign keys or duplicate customer/product data.
invariants: At most one row exists per customer/product pair.
PostgreSQL is durable source of wishlist truth.
No production or real customer data is used in migration smoke.
purpose: Create the minimal durable wishlist storage boundary required by all later behavior.
success_outcome: Wishlist items persist uniquely by customer/product through the supported Medusa module and PostgreSQL path.
anti_goals: Do not implement API routes, auth, workflows, or storefront UI.
Do not link directly to core tables with database foreign keys.
Do not add cleanup jobs or events.

stop_conditions: Composite uniqueness cannot be represented in a supported Medusa model/migration.
Persistence smoke cannot exercise real local PostgreSQL.
Implementation requires direct core table mutation.
